description: Combat 18 is a neo-Nazi terrorist organization founded in 1992 in the United Kingdom. It originated as a stewarding group for the British National Party but later split and became openly violent. The group has been linked to murders of immigrants, dissidents, and German politician Walter Lübcke. It has been banned in Canada and Germany and is still active.

country_links.evidence_quote: In late 2010, five members of Combat 18 Australia (among them Jacob Marshall Hort and Bradley Neil Trappitt) were charged over an attack on a mosque in Perth, Western Australia.
country_links.context: Five members of Combat 18 Australia were charged over an attack on a mosque in Perth.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 6 September 2006, the Belgian police arrested 20 members of Combat 18 Flanders.
country_links.context: Belgian police arrested 20 members of Combat 18 Flanders, including soldiers.

country_links.evidence_quote: It originated in the United Kingdom with ties to movements in Canada and the United States.
country_links.context: Combat 18 has ties to movements in Canada and was added to Canada's terrorist list.

country_links.evidence_quote: There exists a Finnish chapter of Combat 18 based in Harjavalta.
country_links.context: There is a Finnish chapter of Combat 18 based in Harjavalta, and they demonstrated in Tampere.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2019 Serbian Combat 18 was discovered to be trafficking firearms to Atomwaffen neo-Nazis selling handguns, assault rifles, grenades and RPG-7s from the Balkans to French neo-Nazis in Marseille.
country_links.context: Combat 18 trafficked firearms to French neo-Nazis in Marseille.

country_links.evidence_quote: Since then, it has spread to other countries, including Germany.
country_links.context: Combat 18 has spread to Germany, has a German branch, and was banned there.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 6 March 2018, eight members of Combat 18 were arrested in Athens, Greece, accused of multiple attacks on immigrants and activists.
country_links.context: Eight members of Combat 18 were arrested in Athens, accused of multiple attacks on immigrants and activists.

country_links.evidence_quote: Between 1998 and 2000, dozens of Combat 18 members in the United Kingdom and Ireland were arrested on various charges during dawn raids by the police.
country_links.context: Combat 18 members in Ireland were arrested during police raids.

country_links.evidence_quote: Combat 18 was the target of renewed law enforcement attention on 10 December 2025 when New Zealand banned the Order of Nine Angles (O9A) as a terrorist organization.
country_links.context: New Zealand banned the Order of Nine Angles, and Combat 18 was instrumental in setting it up.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2019 Serbian Combat 18 was discovered to be trafficking firearms to Atomwaffen neo-Nazis selling handguns, assault rifles, grenades and RPG-7s from the Balkans to French neo-Nazis in Marseille.
country_links.context: Serbian Combat 18 was discovered trafficking firearms to Atomwaffen neo-Nazis.

country_links.evidence_quote: It originated in the United Kingdom with ties to movements in Canada and the United States.
country_links.context: Combat 18 has ties to movements in the United States and has chapters in Illinois, Florida, and Texas.
